title: Rajas con Crema Roasted Pepper Cream

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Rajas con Crema, a creamy Mexican roasted poblano pepper dish, served in a bowl.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, creamy flavors of homemade Rajas con Crema, a classic Mexican vegetarian delight.

description: Rajas con Crema is a beloved Mexican vegetarian dish featuring tender strips of roasted poblano peppers bathed in a rich, creamy sauce. This comforting and flavorful recipe brings the authentic taste of Mexico to your table with simple ingredients and traditional techniques. It makes a wonderful side dish or a delicious filling for tacos and quesadillas.

ingredients.notes: roasted, peeled, seeded, and cut into strips

instructions.title: Roast the Poblano Peppers
instructions.text: Place poblano peppers directly over an open flame on a gas stove or under a broiler, turning frequently until the skin is completely charred and blistered on all sides. This will take about 5-10 minutes.

instructions.title: Peel and Prepare Peppers
instructions.text: Once charred, immediately transfer the hot peppers to a bowl and cover tightly with plastic wrap or place them in a sealed paper bag. Let them steam for 10-15 minutes. This makes the skin easier to remove. Once cooled, peel off the skin, remove the stems and seeds, then slice the peppers into thin strips (rajas).

instructions.title: Sauté Onion and Garlic
instructions.text: Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or comal over medium heat. Add the thinly sliced white onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Add Peppers and Corn
instructions.text: Stir in the prepared poblano strips and corn kernels to the skillet. Cook for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the corn to warm through. If using frozen corn, ensure it is fully thawed.

instructions.title: Create the Creamy Sauce
instructions.text: Pour in the Mexican crema and milk. Stir well to combine, ensuring all the ingredients are coated in the creamy sauce. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, but do not boil.

instructions.title: Simmer and Season
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to low and let the Rajas con Crema simmer for 5-10 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly and the flavors to deepen. Season generously with salt and black pepper to taste. Adjust seasoning as needed.

instructions.title: Serve Warm
instructions.text: Serve the Rajas con Crema warm as a side dish, a filling for tacos, quesadillas, or enchiladas, or alongside rice and beans. Garnish with a sprinkle of queso fresco or cotija cheese, if desired.

tips: For an authentic smoky flavor, roast your poblanos over an open flame on a gas stove or grill until completely charred. This method imparts a depth of flavor that baking cannot replicate.
If Mexican crema is unavailable, you can use a mix of sour cream and a little milk to achieve a similar consistency and tang. Heavy cream can also be used for a richer, less tangy sauce.

Rajas con Crema can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. It reheats well on the stovetop over low heat, adding a splash of milk if the sauce has thickened too much.
article:
Rajas con Crema Roasted Pepper Cream: A Mexican Vegetarian Delight

In the vibrant tapestry of Mexican cuisine, certain dishes stand out for their comforting warmth, rich flavors, and undeniable versatility. Among these cherished recipes is **Rajas con Crema**, a dish that beautifully marries the smoky essence of roasted poblano peppers with a luscious, creamy sauce. This Mexican vegetarian staple is more than just a side dish; it's a testament to how simple, fresh ingredients can be transformed into something truly extraordinary. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook looking to explore new flavors, Rajas con Crema offers a delicious and satisfying experience that’s both easy to prepare and incredibly rewarding.

## What is Rajas con Crema?

At its heart, "Rajas con Crema" translates directly to "strips with cream." The "rajas" refer to strips of roasted poblano peppers, which are the star of this dish. Poblanos are known for their mild to medium heat, earthy flavor, and beautiful dark green hue. When roasted, their skin blisters and chars, infusing them with a deep, smoky aroma that is utterly irresistible.

The "con crema" aspect typically involves a rich, dairy-based sauce. Traditional Mexican crema is often used, offering a slightly tangy, less thick consistency than sour cream. However, sour cream, heavy cream, or a combination can also be employed to achieve that signature creamy texture. Often, onions and garlic are sautéed to form an aromatic base, and sometimes corn kernels or cheese (like Oaxaca or Monterey Jack) are added to enhance the flavor and texture. The result is a savory, subtly spicy, and incredibly comforting dish that’s naturally gluten-free and a fantastic vegetarian option.

## Ingredients for Rajas con Crema

To create authentic **Rajas con Crema**, you'll need a handful of fresh, quality ingredients. The beauty of this dish lies in its simplicity, allowing the core flavors to shine.

*   **Poblano Peppers:** 4-6 large poblano peppers. These are the foundation of the dish, providing the smoky, earthy flavor.
*   **White Onion:** 1 medium white onion, thinly sliced. This adds a sweet, aromatic base.
*   **Garlic:** 3-4 cloves garlic, minced. Essential for depth of flavor.
*   **Oil:** 1-2 tablespoons vegetable oil or olive oil, for sautéing.
*   **Mexican Crema or Heavy Cream:** 1 cup (240ml) Mexican crema, sour cream, or heavy cream. For a tangier flavor, use sour cream; for richness, heavy cream. A mix works wonderfully.
*   **Cheese (Optional):** 1/2 cup shredded Oaxaca cheese, Monterey Jack, or cream cheese, for extra richness and a gooey texture.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To taste.
*   **Optional Additions:**
    *   1/2 cup corn kernels (fresh, frozen, or canned) for sweetness and texture.
    *   A sprig of epazote (a traditional Mexican herb) for authentic flavor.
    *   A splash of milk or chicken broth to adjust consistency.
    *   Fresh cilantro for garnish.

## How to Make Rajas con Crema (Step-by-Step Instructions)

Mastering **Rajas con Crema** is simpler than you might think, and the process is incredibly rewarding.

**Step 1: Roast the Poblano Peppers**
This is the most crucial step for developing the dish's signature smoky flavor.
*   **Method 1 (Open Flame):** Place poblano peppers directly over an open gas burner flame, turning frequently with tongs until the skin is completely charred and blistered on all sides.
*   **Method 2 (Broiler):** Place peppers on a baking sheet under a hot broiler. Broil for 5-7 minutes per side, turning until evenly charred.
*   **Method 3 (Oven):** Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Place peppers on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes, turning halfway, until softened and slightly charred.
Once roasted, immediately transfer the hot peppers to a bowl and cover tightly with plastic wrap, or place them in a paper bag and seal. Let them steam for 15-20 minutes. This steaming process loosens the skin, making it easier to peel.

**Step 2: Prepare the Peppers**
Once cooled enough to handle, peel the charred skin from the poblanos. Don't worry if a few small bits of char remain; they add to the smoky flavor. Slice each pepper lengthwise, open it up, and carefully remove the seeds and veins. Discard them. Cut the peeled, deseeded peppers into 1/4-inch wide strips (the "rajas").

**Step 3: Sauté Aromatics**
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or pot over medium heat. Add the thinly sliced white onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

**Step 4: Combine and Simmer**
Add the prepared poblano strips (rajas) to the skillet with the onions and garlic. Stir well to combine. Pour in the Mexican crema (or heavy cream/sour cream). If using cheese, stir it in now. Add salt and pepper to taste. If using corn or epazote, add them at this stage. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook for 5-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened slightly and the flavors have melded beautifully. If the sauce becomes too thick, you can add a splash of milk or broth to reach your desired consistency.

**Step 5: Taste and Serve**
Taste the Rajas con Crema and adjust seasonings as needed. Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.

## Tips for the Best Rajas con Crema

To ensure your **Rajas con Crema** is nothing short of spectacular, keep these tips in mind:

*   **Don't Skip the Roasting:** The smoky flavor from properly roasted poblanos is non-negotiable. It’s what defines the dish.
*   **Steam for Easy Peeling:** Steaming the peppers after roasting is key. It makes peeling effortless and prevents you from losing too much pepper flesh.
*   **Control the Heat:** Poblanos are generally mild, but their heat can vary. To reduce any potential spiciness, remove all seeds and white membranes. For a bit more kick, leave a few in, or add a slice of jalapeño or serrano during the roasting process.
*   **Quality Cream Matters:** Use a good quality Mexican crema or your preferred dairy. The creaminess is central to the dish's appeal.
*   **Simmer Gently:** Allow the flavors to meld over a gentle simmer. This develops a richer, more complex taste.
*   **Season Generously:** Taste and adjust salt and pepper throughout the cooking process. A pinch of cumin or a dash of smoked paprika can also enhance the depth.

## Serving Suggestions for Rajas con Crema

The beauty of **Rajas con Crema** lies in its incredible versatility. It can be a star player or a supporting act in a wide array of meals.

*   **Tacos and Quesadillas:** This is perhaps the most popular way to enjoy rajas con crema. Fill warm tortillas with the mixture, perhaps adding a sprinkle of extra cheese and a dollop of guacamole.
*   **Enchilada or Burrito Filling:** Use it as a delicious vegetarian filling for enchiladas or burritos.
*   **Side Dish:** Serve alongside grilled meats (like carne asada or pollo asado), rice, and beans for a complete Mexican feast.
*   **Breakfast Staple:** Pair it with scrambled eggs, or fold it into an omelet for a hearty and flavorful breakfast.
*   **Topping for Tostadas:** Spread a generous amount over crispy tostadas.
*   **Dip:** Serve warm with tortilla chips as a satisfying dip.
*   **Stuffed Bell Peppers:** Mix with rice and stuff into bell peppers for a comforting baked meal.

## Rajas con Crema Variations & Substitutions

While the classic **Rajas con Crema** is divine, feel free to explore variations and substitutions to suit your taste or dietary needs.

*   **Vegan Rajas con Crema:** Replace dairy cream with cashew cream, full-fat coconut milk (note: this will impart a slight coconut flavor), or a plant-based sour cream. Omit cheese or use a vegan shredded cheese alternative.
*   **Add Vegetables:** Incorporate other vegetables like corn kernels (as mentioned), sliced mushrooms, zucchini, or even diced potatoes for a heartier dish.
*   **Spicier Kick:** For those who love heat, roast a jalapeño or serrano pepper along with the poblanos, then finely mince and add to the dish.
*   **Cheesy Variations:** Experiment with different cheeses. Cream cheese adds extra richness and thickness, while queso fresco crumbles beautifully on top.
*   **Lighter Version:** Use evaporated milk or a mix of milk and light sour cream instead of heavy cream for a less caloric option.
*   **Smoked Paprika:** A pinch of smoked paprika can further enhance the smoky notes if you find your peppers didn't get quite enough char.

Rajas con Crema is more than just a recipe; it's an experience of robust, comforting flavors that transport you straight to the heart of Mexico. Its ease of preparation, combined with its rich taste and adaptability, makes it a must-try for anyone seeking a delicious and satisfying vegetarian dish. Dive in and discover the magic of roasted peppers and cream!
